channel.title: KillBait RSS Feed
channel.description: KillBait - News highlights delivered clearly and responsibly—no clickbait, no sensationalism

item.title: Papunya residents take legal action against NT Government over unsafe housing conditions
item.description: Residents of the Aboriginal community of Papunya, located in the Northern Territory, have initiated legal action against the NT Government, claiming that decades of neglect and outdated housing are pu...

item.title: UK and France agree expanded funding and enforcement plan to curb Channel crossings
item.description: The United Kingdom and France have agreed a new three-year deal worth £662 million aimed at reducing the number of migrants crossing the English Channel in small boats. The agreement includes deployin...

item.title: Gauteng nurse admits insurance fraud involving stepdaughter, denies murder charge
item.description: A Gauteng nurse, Sithembile Xulu, has pleaded guilty to defrauding insurance companies by taking out multiple life insurance and funeral policies in the name of her stepdaughter, Busisiwe Nxumalo, wit...

item.title: Ongoing technical issues frustrate Sassa beneficiaries using eLife Certification system

item.title: How Table Tennis Can Help People with Parkinson’s Disease
item.description: This article discusses an innovative initiative that uses table tennis as a therapeutic activity for individuals with Parkinson’s disease, spearheaded by Fundación Red Parkinson. Parkinson’s is a neur...

item.title: FG sets up committee to review and restructure Nigeria’s security agencies
item.description: Federal Government don set up one inter-ministerial committee to look into how security and para-military agencies dey operate across Nigeria. This move, as dem explain am, na to reduce the plenty age...

item.title: North Korea and Russia prepare to open new cross-border road bridge to boost trade and travel
item.description: North Korea and Russia are close to completing construction of their first road bridge linking the two countries, marking a significant step in strengthening their political and economic ties. The 850...

item.title: Senate starts screening of Muttaqha Darma for ministerial appointment
item.description: Today, the Senate don begin the screening process for Muttaqha Darma, wey President Bola Tinubu nominate as minister for the Federal Republic of Nigeria. Darma enter the hallowed chamber around 12:32 ...

item.title: Law Society of Kenya Calls for Probe into Killing of Advocate Tom Imbukwa
item.description: There is heavy shock and anger within Kenya’s legal fraternity following the killing of advocate Tom Ouya Imbukwa. According to the Law Society of Kenya (LSK), Imbukwa was allegedly assaulted, torture...

item.title: Jonathan Green pays tribute to James Valentine following his death
item.description: James Valentine, a beloved broadcaster at the ABC, has passed away at the age of 64 after battling cancer. He had been an integral part of the Sydney radio scene for over 25 years, hosting the Afterno...

item.title: Lyrid meteor shower guide: when to watch and what the weather forecast looks like
item.description: The Lyrid meteor shower, one of the oldest recorded meteor showers with observations going back over 2,700 years, typically peaks around late April each year. In 2026 it is expected to produce roughly...

item.title: South Africa’s Focus Areas for Strengthening Environmental Protection
item.description: On Earth Day, attention has turned to South Africa’s environmental performance and the country’s efforts toward a just energy transition. Key priorities include moving from awareness to measurable acc...

item.title: Nine workers killed in armed assault at copper-gold mining site in Chagai, Balochistan
item.description: At least nine people lost their lives in a tragic armed attack at a copper and gold mining project site in Balochistan’s Chagai district, according to local authorities and official confirmations. The...

item.title: Government proposes NDIS changes affecting 160,000 participants

item.title: Victims' families welcome ICC confirmation of crimes against Duterte over drug war killings
